Deploy SGSC-AI protocol to analyze the supply chain risk and uncover the true industry role of Longhe Electrical Equipment Co., Ltd..

**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Longhe Electrical Equipment Co., Ltd. presents a textbook case of a massive cross-provincial corporate relocation coupled with a radical business pivot. Originally established in Heilongjiang as 'Daqing Longhe Petroleum Technology Development Co., Ltd.' (an entity deeply rooted in Daqing's oilfield supply chain), it was later relocated to Zhongshan, Guangdong. Concurrently, it changed its name to suggest 'Electrical Equipment' manufacturing. The company boasts an inflated registered capital of 50M RMB, a bewilderingly expansive business scope covering everything from oil drilling equipment and boilers to apparel, stationery, and IT services, yet its 2024 insured employee count is merely 3. An operation spanning heavy machinery, chemical processing, elevator manufacturing, and general retail cannot function with 3 employees in a 'Zone 107' address in a Torch Development Zone village. This entity is highly likely a repurposed trading shell or an invoice/bidding vehicle holding legacy oilfield and special equipment licenses (e.g., TS2723080-2019).

**Risk Profile Summary:**
Trust Tier: **L1_REGISTERED** (Score: 72)
Operational Status: ACTIVE (ON PAPER) / DORMANT MANUFACTURING & ACTIVE TRADING SHELL
